recipes.title: Mofongo
recipes.description: Puerto Rican mash of fried green plantains pounded with garlic, olive oil and chicharrón, served as a dome with broth.
steps: Peel plantains and slice into 2 cm rounds.
Fry rounds in oil at 170°C for 6 minutes until tender but not browned; drain.
In a pilón (mortar), mash garlic with olive oil and salt.
Add hot plantain pieces in batches and pound, mixing in chicharrón as you go.
Pack the mash into a small bowl and invert onto a plate to form a dome; serve with warm broth on the side.

recipes.title: Arroz con Gandules
recipes.description: Puerto Rican rice cooked with sofrito, pigeon peas and pork in achiote-tinted broth, the island's signature side.
steps: Render salt pork in a caldero, then add ham and brown lightly.
Stir in sofrito, achiote oil and tomato sauce; cook 2 minutes.
Add gandules and alcaparrado, then the rice and toast 1 minute.
Pour in broth, season with salt, bring to a boil and stir once.
Cover, lower heat and cook 25 minutes until liquid is absorbed; fluff with a fork before serving.

recipes.title: Pernil
recipes.description: Puerto Rican garlic-marinated roast pork shoulder with crackling skin (cuerito), the centrepiece of holiday meals.
steps: Crush garlic with salt, oregano, pepper, cumin, oil and vinegar to make adobo.
Loosen the pork skin and pierce the meat all over; rub adobo deep into every cut and under the skin.
Wrap and refrigerate at least 24 hours.
Roast skin-side up at 160°C, uncovered, for 4 hours, basting with pan juices.
Increase to 230°C and roast 30 minutes until the skin (cuerito) is crackling.
Rest 20 minutes, then carve, breaking the cuerito into shards to serve on top.

recipes.title: Pasteles
recipes.description: Puerto Rican green-banana and root masa stuffed with seasoned pork, wrapped in plantain leaves and boiled.
steps: Stew pork with sofrito, tomato sauce, alcaparrado and a little water for 45 minutes; cool.
Grate green bananas, yautía, plantain and pumpkin, then beat in milk, achiote oil and salt to a creamy masa.
Soften plantain leaves over a flame and cut into 25 cm rectangles, lightly oiled.
Spread masa on each leaf, top with a spoon of pork stew, fold into a flat rectangular parcel.
Tie pasteles in pairs with twine and boil in salted water for 60 minutes.
Drain, unwrap and serve with rice and beans.

recipes.title: Alcapurria
recipes.description: Puerto Rican fritter of grated green banana and yautía masa stuffed with picadillo and deep-fried.
steps: Cook ground beef with sofrito, cumin, tomato sauce and alcaparrado for 15 minutes; cool.
Grate green bananas and yautía finely, mix with achiote oil and salt to a smooth orange masa.
Spread a strip of masa on a square of greased plastic, top with picadillo, then fold into a torpedo shape.
Heat oil to 170°C and fry alcapurrias 6-8 minutes until deeply golden and crisp.
Drain on paper and serve hot.

recipes.title: Tostones
recipes.description: Puerto Rican twice-fried green plantain rounds smashed flat and salted, served with mojo or as a side.
steps: Peel plantains and slice into 3 cm rounds.
Fry rounds at 160°C for 4 minutes until tender but pale; drain.
Smash each round flat with a tostonera or the bottom of a glass.
Dip flattened rounds briefly in salted garlic water, then drain.
Fry again at 180°C for 2-3 minutes until crisp and deep gold.
Sprinkle with salt and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Bacalaítos
recipes.description: Puerto Rican thin, crispy salt cod fritters from beachside kioskos in Piñones.
steps: Soak cod in cold water 24 hours, changing the water several times; drain, simmer 10 minutes, cool and flake, removing bones.
Whisk flour, baking powder, pepper and water into a thin pancake-like batter.
Stir in sofrito, garlic, cilantro and the flaked cod.
Heat oil to 180°C and ladle thin disks of batter into the oil.
Fry 2-3 minutes per side until lacy and crisp; drain and serve immediately.

recipes.title: Sancocho
recipes.description: Puerto Rican rich beef and root-vegetable stew with corn, plantain and yautía.

ingredients.name: corn on the cob, in rounds

steps: Brown the beef in oil with sofrito until aromatic, then add tomato sauce.
Add water and salt and simmer 60 minutes until beef is becoming tender.
Add yautía, yuca, potato and pumpkin; cook 20 minutes.
Add plantain rounds and corn and cook 15 minutes more until everything is fork-tender.
Finish with cilantro, taste and adjust salt, and serve in deep bowls with white rice.

recipes.title: Tembleque
recipes.description: Puerto Rican silky coconut milk pudding set with cornstarch, dusted with cinnamon for Christmas.
steps: Whisk cornstarch with 200 ml of coconut milk until smooth.
In a saucepan, combine remaining coconut milk, sugar, salt and cinnamon stick; warm until sugar dissolves.
Whisk in the cornstarch slurry and cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thick like pudding, about 8 minutes.
Remove the cinnamon stick and pour into a mould or individual cups.
Chill at least 4 hours, then unmould and dust with ground cinnamon.
